summary: Iteration 7 (P4 refactor verification): server.py split into db.py + services/{pdf,email,invoices}.py. Ran all prior regression suites (test_backend.py 33/34 with the known .user-wrapper false-fail, test_refactor.py 15/15, test_auth_profile_branding.py 22/23 with 1 stale test-file bug, test_delete_and_branding.py 7/7, test_bulk_send.py 14/14+2 skipped) — all match iteration_6 counts when run SERIALLY. The refactor is functionally equivalent: PDF generation (with studio_name+logo — pypdf confirms 'Lakshmi School of Dance' embedded), email dispatch to delivered@resend.dev (status=sent, email_id populated, last_sent_to/last_sent_at set on invoice), password reset round-trip, bulk-send skip semantics, invoice generate response shape, shared invoice/logo endpoints, and all thin wrappers (_generate_invoice_pdf, _filter_by_date, _create_invoice_for_student, _build_invoice_email_html, _origin_from_public_link, _build_email_payload, _dispatch_email, _mark_invoice_sent, _send_password_reset_email, _wa_link) delegate correctly. Final admin password left as 'prashanth' (login 200 verified via curl).

minor.endpoint: python -c 'from services import pdf, email, invoices'
minor.issue: Standalone import of the service modules FAILS with KeyError: 'MONGO_URL'. Root cause: /app/backend/db.py L10 does `client = AsyncIOMotorClient(os.environ['MONGO_URL'])` at MODULE IMPORT time, and services/email.py + services/invoices.py both do `from db import db` at their own module top-level. When importing `services` from a shell that hasn't pre-loaded /app/backend/.env, MONGO_URL is unset and the import chain crashes. Under supervisor this works because server.py loads dotenv on line 5 BEFORE importing db/services — but the problem statement's stated sanity check `python -c 'from services import pdf, email, invoices'` (from /app/backend) does not pass without prepending `from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv('.env');` or exporting MONGO_URL/DB_NAME in the shell. Recommend deferring the client init to a lazy factory (e.g., a `get_db()` function) so the modules are import-time pure and testable in isolation. This is the ONLY refactor-related regression.

action_items: OPTIONAL (refactor cleanup): change /app/backend/db.py to lazy-init the Motor client (module-level `client = None; def get_client(): global client; if client is None: client = AsyncIOMotorClient(os.environ['MONGO_URL']); return client`). Then services/email.py + services/invoices.py can `from db import get_db` and call it inside async functions. This would let `python -c 'from services import pdf, email, invoices'` succeed as claimed in the review request, and it also keeps env-var reads at CALL time (aligning with the design note in the problem statement).

OPTIONAL (test-file bug, unrelated to refactor): /app/backend/tests/test_auth_profile_branding.py::TestCredentialsRotation::test_old_creds_fail (L75-77) posts {email: 'lpathreya@gmail.com', password: 'prashanth'} and asserts 401. But 'prashanth' IS the current admin password — this test is stale and always fails when run serially. It probably predates the credentials-rotation and should either (a) use the truly-old creds like teacher@dance.com/dance123, or (b) be removed. It only 'passed' in prior iterations because a parallel worker race in TestChangePassword briefly rotated the password.

OPTIONAL (test infra): pytest.ini uses `-n 2 --dist loadscope` which puts TestBulkPreview + TestBulkSend on separate xdist workers. Both re-run the module-scope autouse `seed_students` fixture whose teardown deletes ALL TEST_-prefixed rows — so worker A's teardown races worker B's still-running tests, wiping the fixtures under it. Symptoms: TestBulkSend.test_skip_both_channels_when_unreachable and test_no_student_ids_only_outstanding fail intermittently. Same class-split race causes test_delete_and_branding.py's module-scope session fixture to see a login-401 when TestChangePassword.test_change_and_verify_flow on another worker is mid-flight. Suggest: (a) rename `seed_students` to session-scoped + guard teardown behind an env flag, OR (b) make the two shared-state test files single-worker via a `pytestmark = pytest.mark.xdist_group('admin_shared')` group, OR (c) drop `--dist loadscope` and use `--dist loadfile`.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1102 lines (down from 1409) — good progress on the P4 line-count goal.

BEHAVIOUR CHANGE (documented and expected per problem statement, but flagging explicitly): server.py L849-853 now ALWAYS skips students with balance_due<=0 in bulk-send, even when student_ids is provided. This closes the iter6 minor issue and matches the review request's spec ('students with balance_due<=0 are always skipped'), but it IS an observable behaviour change over iter6 code — not a pure no-op refactor. The comment in the code correctly documents the change.

server.py still declares `import reportlab.platypus`, `reportlab.lib.pagesizes`, `reportlab.lib.colors`, `reportlab.lib.styles`, `reportlab.lib.units`, and `Image as RLImage` at the top (L26-30) but ONLY uses `io` (via StreamingResponse buffer) and delegates all PDF work to services/pdf.py. Those reportlab imports are now DEAD in server.py — remove them to keep the module clean. Same for the `import httpx` at L15 which is commented as 'kept for backward-compat with previous callers' but is only used inside the `except httpx.HTTPStatusError` handler in send_invoice — that use is legitimate, keep the httpx import but remove the reportlab ones.

db.py reads env-vars at IMPORT time (L10-11). This defeats the design goal noted in the problem statement about read-at-call-time env access. services/email.py's `_email_key()` and `_from_name()` correctly defer, but db.py itself does not. Move the client construction into a lazy factory (see action items).

services/email.py L14 hard-imports `db` at module level. If db.py is made lazy, this needs to become a function-level import or a lazy handle. Currently: email_service.py can't be imported without a working MONGO_URL, which is what causes the standalone-import failure above.
services/invoices.py L10 same issue: `from db import db` at module top-level.

server.py L15: `import httpx  # noqa: F401` — actually httpx IS used at L1020 in the `except httpx.HTTPStatusError` clause. The noqa comment is misleading; either the previous refactor pass thought it unused or the comment is stale. Either way, low priority.

server.py L42: `EMERGENT_KEY = os.environ.get('EMERGENT_LLM_KEY')` is read at import time, but the equivalent env-var read in services/email.py (`_email_key()`) is deferred. Same inconsistency for `EMAIL_KEY` at L43 (used by send_invoice to gate 503) and `EMAIL_FROM_NAME` at L44. Consistency would help — either all lazy or all eager.

server.py L661-665 `_create_invoice_for_student` wrapper injects `ser_class`/`ser_payment`/`ser_student` as keyword args on every call. This works but couples the wrapper's call-site knowledge to the services module. Alternative: expose the serializers via a service-side registration function or pass them once at app startup.

Nothing in services/*.py is unit-testable without a live DB (both email.py and invoices.py depend on the imported `db` handle). Consider inverting the dependency: pass `db` as a parameter into async functions that need it.

server.py wrappers dropped type hints (intentional per problem statement note). Fine, but this DOES hide the signature at the call site — IDE completion falls back to *args in some editors. Not blocking.

success_rate.backend: 100% for the refactor-scope suites when run serially: test_backend.py 33/34 (known .user false-fail unchanged), test_refactor.py 15/15, test_auth_profile_branding.py 22/23 (stale test_old_creds_fail unrelated to refactor), test_delete_and_branding.py 7/7, test_bulk_send.py 14/14 executed + 2 skipped. Cumulative refactor-relevant: 91/93 (98%).
success_rate.frontend: not tested this iteration (backend-only per review request)

test_credentials: lpathreya@gmail.com / prashanth — verified via curl POST /api/auth/login → 200 at end of run. Password preserved.
seed_data_creation: test_bulk_send.py autouse fixture creates 5 TEST_bulk_* students with a 2026-02-10 class each and deletes them (plus their generated invoices) at module teardown. test_refactor.py creates a TEST_Refactor Student + classes + invoice and cleans them at end. All TEST_-prefixed rows removed at end-of-run (verified: GET /api/students returns no TEST_ prefixes).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor is behaviour-preserving with two footnotes: (1) bulk-send now always skips balance_due<=0 (this MATCHES the review-request spec but is technically a behaviour change vs iter6), and (2) standalone `python -c 'from services import ...'` fails because db.py reads MONGO_URL at import time — the fix is a lazy factory in db.py. All wrappers exist: _generate_invoice_pdf, _filter_by_date, _create_invoice_for_student, _build_invoice_email_html, _origin_from_public_link, _build_email_payload, _dispatch_email, _mark_invoice_sent, _send_password_reset_email, _wa_link — all delegate correctly. Test-infra note: pytest.ini's `-n 2 --dist loadscope` splits classes across xdist workers, which races on the shared admin user for password-change tests and on TEST_ fixture teardown — always add `-o addopts='-n 0'` for deterministic runs. Container clock: 2026-07-18. delivered@resend.dev works. Admin password 'prashanth' preserved.
rca of the issue: The only new backend issue (standalone import failure) is caused by db.py evaluating `os.environ['MONGO_URL']` and `os.environ['DB_NAME']` at module IMPORT time (lines 10-11), while services/email.py and services/invoices.py transitively import db.py at their own module top-level. Reproduction: `cd /app/backend && env -u MONGO_URL python -c 'from services import pdf, email, invoices'` → KeyError: 'MONGO_URL'. Mitigation (do not apply — main agent's call): defer client construction to a lazy factory, e.g. `def get_db(): return _client_singleton()` and update services to call it inside async functions. Under supervisor this bug is invisible because server.py loads dotenv on line 5 before any service import.
